per_case.user_input: What spatter index value triggers the initial alarm on WR-401 (not the auto-quarantine threshold)?
retrieved_contexts:
[From SOP-WR-401-welding-robot.txt]:
NOTE: After spatter shield clean, sensor may read falsely high
  for up to 30 minutes. If visual weld quality is normal and
  index is below 4.0, monitor for 30 minutes before escalating.

3.2 AUTO-QUARANTINE ALARM (Spatter Index ≥ 5.0)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Step 1: Line pauses automatically. Do NOT override.
  Step 2: Tag and physically separate all panels produced in the
          last 10 minutes for quality inspection.
  Step 3: Inspect contact tip, wire liner, and spatter shield.
  Step 4: Replace contact tip. Clean shield.
  Step 5: Check wire liner for blockage — replace if resistance felt.
  Step 6: Run 5 test welds before returning to production.
  Step 7: Notify shift supervisor and quality team.
  Step 8: Complete NCR if more than 6 panels quarantined.

[From NCR-WR-401-2024-04-spatter-recurrence.txt]:
NON-CONFORMANCE REPORT
Document: NCR-WR-401-2024-04
Title: WR-401 Recurring Spatter Alarms — Wire Liner Root Cause
Equipment Tag: WR-401
Line: Line 4 — Body Panel Assembly
Plant Site: Northgate Automotive
Date Raised: 03-Apr-2024
Raised By: A. Kovacs, Maintenance Technician
Approved By: J. Hartley, Production Manager

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1. NON-CONFORMANCE DESCRIPTION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

WR-401 recorded 3 spatter alarms within a 4-day period
(29-Mar, 01-Apr, 02-Apr). Per SOP-WR-401 Section 3.3,
recurring spatter within 7 days requires investigation and
maintenance inspection rather than operator clear-and-continue.

Panels affected: approx. 14 panels quarantined across 3 events.
All quarantined panels subsequently passed visual QC inspection.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
2. ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

2024-04-19 — Night shift — Line 4 — at 22:15 — [Alarm] — Equipment: WR-401 — Spatter index alarm — reading 3.8 — Action: Cleaned spatter shield. Spatter index reduced to 2.1 after clean. — Operator: J. Malik — Status: Resolved
2024-04-19 — Night shift — Line 4 — at 23:40 — [Alarm] — Equipment: WR-401 — Spatter index alarm — reading 3.9 — Action: Replaced contact tip. Spatter reduced to 2.4. — Operator: J. Malik — Status: Resolved
2024-04-20 — Night shift — Line 4 — at 01:20 — [Maintenance] — Equipment: WR-401 — Preventive check — wire feed tension — Action: Checked wire feed drive roll pressure. Adjusted to 3.5 bar. All normal. — Operator: J. Malik — Status: Complete
2024-04-20 — Night shift — Line 4 — at 02:10 — [Alarm] — Equipment: WR-401 — Weld quality alarm — spatter index 4.8. Line auto-paused. — Action: Inspected wire liner — resistance felt at 5m. Notified maintenance for morning. Cleaned shield and tip. Resumed after 12 mins. — Operator: J. Malik — Status: Resolved — maintenance follow-up required

[From SOP-WR-401-welding-robot.txt]:
Key specifications:
  - Wire type:        ER70S-6 high-tensile (structural panels)
  - Wire diameter:    1.2 mm
  - Shielding gas:    75% Argon / 25% CO2 at 18 L/min
  - Spatter index alarm threshold:      3.5
  - Spatter index auto-quarantine:      5.0
  - Weld dwell time (B-pillar):         420 ms
  - Weld current (B-pillar):            9.2 kA
  - Tip-to-work distance (nominal):     15 mm ± 1 mm

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
2. PRE-SHIFT STARTUP CHECKLIST
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Before enabling robot for production, operator MUST verify:

[From SOP-WR-401-welding-robot.txt]:
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3. ALARM RESPONSE PROCEDURES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

3.1 SPATTER ALARM (Spatter Index 3.5 – 4.9)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
  Step 1: Do NOT stop the line immediately. Note the reading.
  Step 2: Check spatter shield — clean if contaminated.
  Step 3: Inspect contact tip — replace if worn.
  Step 4: Verify gas flow at 18 L/min.
  Step 5: Run 3 test welds. If index drops below 3.5, resume.
  Step 6: If index remains above 3.5 after tip and shield clean,
          escalate to maintenance — possible wire feed issue.
  Step 7: Log all actions in shift log with timestamp.

  NOTE: After spatter shield clean, sensor may read falsely high
  for up to 30 minutes. If visual weld quality is normal and
  index is below 4.0, monitor for 30 minutes before escalating.
per_case.response: The spatter index value that triggers the initial alarm on WR-401 is 3.5.
per_case.reference: The initial spatter index alarm threshold for WR-401 is 3.5. When the spatter index exceeds 3.5 a quality alarm is triggered. A separate higher threshold of 5.0 triggers automatic panel quarantine. These are two distinct thresholds: 3.5 for the initial alarm, 5.0 for auto-quarantine.

per_case.user_input: After replacing the wire liner on WR-401, what does the SOP require the operator to do before resuming production?
retrieved_contexts:
[From SOP-WR-401-welding-robot.txt]:
After any wire liner replacement, the following MUST be observed:

  - Run 50 m burn-in before production. Wire feed inconsistency
    during the first 50 m is NORMAL and expected.
  - First 2 hours after liner change: wire feed hesitation is
    acceptable. Do not raise fault or alarm.
  - After burn-in complete, verify tip-to-work distance and run
    3 quality test welds before resuming full production.
  - Log liner replacement in maintenance record with date and
    wire type used.

Wire liner replacement interval: every 6 weeks or at first sign
of resistance. Do not wait for feed failure before replacing.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
5. WIRE SPECIFICATION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

WR-401 is approved for structural panel welding ONLY.

  APPROVED:    ER70S-6 high-tensile wire (min yield 490 MPa)
  NOT APPROVED: ER70S-2 standard wire

[From NCR-WR-401-2024-04-spatter-recurrence.txt]:
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3. IMMEDIATE CONTAINMENT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  - 14 quarantined panels inspected by QC.
  - Result: all panels pass — weld quality within spec.
  - No panels scrapped. Quarantine released.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
4. PREVENTIVE ACTION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  - Wire spool storage moved away from washing station (completed
    05-Apr — Facilities).
  - Condensation shield fitted to Line 4 storage cabinet
    (completed 08-Apr — Facilities).
  - Wire liner inspection added to weekly check for WR-401
    (added to SOP-WR-401 pending next revision).
  - Monitor liner lifespan over next 4 service cycles.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
5. RECURRENCE NOTE — PREVIOUS NCR
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

[From SOP-WR-401-welding-robot.txt]:
Before enabling robot for production, operator MUST verify:

  □ Wire spool level — minimum 2 kg remaining. Replace if below.
  □ Contact tip condition — replace if visibly worn or spattered.
  □ Spatter shield clean — wipe with dry cloth. Do NOT use solvent.
  □ Gas flow confirmed at 18 L/min — check rotameter on rear panel.
  □ Tip-to-work distance check — use calibration gauge, must be
    15 mm ± 1 mm. Adjust TCP if outside tolerance.
  □ No fault indicators on HMI — clear any latched warnings.
  □ Safety cage door closed and interlocked — green light on panel.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3. ALARM RESPONSE PROCEDURES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

[From SOP-WR-401-welding-robot.txt]:
WR-401 is approved for structural panel welding ONLY.

  APPROVED:    ER70S-6 high-tensile wire (min yield 490 MPa)
  NOT APPROVED: ER70S-2 standard wire

ER70S-2 is approved for non-structural body panels only.
DO NOT substitute ER70S-2 on any structural panel weld
(B-pillar, roof panel, floor crossmember).

Using incorrect wire on structural welds is a SAFETY VIOLATION
and requires immediate NCR and engineering review.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
6. END OF SHIFT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  □ Set robot to HOME position via HMI.
  □ Record panel count, alarm count, and any incidents in shift log.
  □ Inspect and clean spatter shield.
  □ Note wire spool level for incoming operator.
  □ Report any open issues to incoming shift supervisor.

END OF DOCUMENT — SOP-WR-401 Rev 3.2

per_case.response:
Before resuming production after replacing the wire liner, the SOP requires the operator to:

1. Run 50 m burn-in before production. Wire feed inconsistency during the first 50 m is NORMAL and expected.
2. First 2 hours after liner change: wire feed hesitation is acceptable. Do not raise fault or alarm.
3. After burn-in complete, verify tip-to-work distance and run 3 quality test welds before resuming full production.
4. Log liner replacement in maintenance record with date and wire type used.
per_case.reference: Wire feed stuttering after a liner replacement is expected behaviour during the burn-in period. The SOP for WR-401 requires a mandatory burn-in procedure after any wire liner replacement. The operator should complete the burn-in procedure before resuming production. This is LOW criticality — expected post-maintenance behaviour, not an equipment failure.
